---
title: "Lambert72 to Lambert 08"
description: "Realign transformed geometries after Lambert72 -> Lambert08 conversion"
author: "Karel Dieussaert"
date: "2026-01-14"
categories: [Lambert72/08, Transformation]
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
jupyter: python3
---
# Lambert72/08 transformation
This example shows a two-step workflow for CRS migration scenarios:

1. Align in Lambert72 (`EPSG:31370`) to create a clean baseline.
2. Transform geometry to Lambert08 (`EPSG:3812`) and re-align to remove transformation-induced offsets.

## Why this is important

When geometries are transformed between CRS systems, small coordinate deviations can appear. A final `brdr` alignment step ensures boundaries follow the target reference layer precisely.

## What to look at in the output

- The final aligned Lambert08 geometry (`result`).
- The corrected deviations (`result_diff`), which show what was adjusted after transformation.

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Alignment after Lambert72/08-transformation"
{{< include ../../examples/example_lambert72_to_lambert08.py >}}
```
